Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Microscopes Can See Video on a LaserDisc 56 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Pushing and Pulling: Three reactivity algorithms 36 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Artificial-life: A simple (300 lines of code) reproduction of Computational Life 16 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. FrameBook 305 upvotes, 60 comments
  5. LibreOffice Writer now supports Markdown 215 upvotes, 40 comments
  6. Z80 Sans – a disassembler in a font (2024) 45 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. Rijksmuseum researchers discover new painting by Rembrandt van Rijn 130 upvotes, 28 comments
  8. What if the Apple ][ had run on Field-Sequential? 68 upvotes, 14 comments
  9. Agent Safehouse – macOS-native sandboxing for local agents 96 upvotes, 23 comments
  10. Every single board computer I tested in 2025 38 upvotes, 7 comments
  11. I made a programming language with M&Ms 23 upvotes, 4 comments
  12. LLM Writing Tropes.md 305 upvotes, 140 comments
  13. Notes on writing Rust-based Wasm 182 upvotes, 79 comments
  14. Log messages are mostly for the people operating your software 82 upvotes, 34 comments
  15. Show HN: I built a real-time OSINT dashboard pulling 15 live global feeds 86 upvotes, 45 comments
  16. The changing goalposts of AGI and timelines 303 upvotes, 190 comments
  17. Beagle, a source code management system that stores AST trees 72 upvotes, 37 comments
  18. WSL Manager 51 upvotes, 26 comments
  19. Lil Finder Guy 18 upvotes, 6 comments
  20. Ask HN: Please restrict new accounts from posting 280 upvotes, 194 comments
  21. SQG (SQL to Code Generator) Now with Java Streams and List Type Support 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Blacksky AppView 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. We should revisit literate programming in the agent era 35 upvotes, 20 comments
  24. Why can't you tune your guitar? (2019) 143 upvotes, 111 comments
  25. Ask HN: How to be alone? 151 upvotes, 134 comments
  26. Living human brain cells play DOOM on a CL1 [video] 110 upvotes, 97 comments
  27. My “grand vision” for Rust 85 upvotes, 76 comments
  28. My Homelab Setup 85 upvotes, 82 comments
  29. Show HN: Skir – like Protocol Buffer but better 42 upvotes, 38 comments
  30. A basket of new fruit varieties is coming your way 30 upvotes, 31 comments

Last generated 6:00am, 9 March 2026. Source here.
